data.summary: A mesmerizing blend of ultra-modern technology, neon-lit skyscrapers, and ancient, tranquil shrines, Japan offers an unforgettable journey through bustling cities and scenic landscapes.

categories.title: Flights
categories.subtitle: Return economy flights from Auckland to Tokyo

categories.subtitle: Mid-range business hotels and a traditional Ryokan stay

lineItems.label: Traditional Ryokan
lineItems.value: 1 night in Hakone with Kaiseki dinner, $900 total

categories.title: Food & Drink

lineItems.label: Temples & Historic Sites

categories.title: Local Transport
categories.subtitle: Bullet trains, metro passes, and local transport cards

lineItems.label: Shinkansen (Bullet Train)
lineItems.value: Tokyo to Kyoto round trip, ~$220 per person

lineItems.label: IC Cards (Suica/Pasmo)
lineItems.value: Subways and buses, ~$120 loaded per person

lineItems.label: Luggage Forwarding
lineItems.value: Takkyubin service, ~$30 per bag between cities

tips: Purchase an eSIM or pocket Wi-Fi at the airport for seamless navigation using Google Maps.
Always carry cash (Yen) as many traditional restaurants, temple ticket booths, and local vendors do not accept international credit cards.
Use luggage forwarding services (Takkyubin) to send large suitcases between Tokyo and Kyoto so you do not struggle on crowded trains.
Download the 'Japan Travel by NAVITIME' app for accurate subway, local train, and Shinkansen route planning.
Observe local etiquette: do not eat while walking, keep your phone on silent on public transit, and do not tip at restaurants.
data.visaInfo: New Zealand citizens traveling to Japan for tourism do not require a visa for stays of up to 90 days, provided they hold a valid passport and a return ticket.
data.bestTimeToVisit: Spring (March to May) for the iconic cherry blossoms, or Autumn (September to November) for pleasant, mild temperatures and stunning autumn foliage.
